question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: prune(RecordFileIterator)] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that causes the transactionOperations.executeWithoutResult() to time out at the database level but not at the application level, resulting in the DB rolling back the transaction while the Java code continues executing the forEach loop and updating counters as if the deletions succeeded?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: prune()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that causes findLatestWithOffset() to return a record file with a consensusEnd of Long.MAX_VALUE or near-overflow value due to data corruption during the partition, causing the RecordFileIterator to compute endTimestamp = current.getConsensusEnd() + batchPeriod with a long overflow, resulting in a negative endTimestamp being passed to repository.prune() and deleting all historical data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: hasNext()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that results in recordFileRepository.findNextBetween() returning Optional.empty() prematurely due to a read timeout, causing the iterator to terminate early and set current=null, while a subsequent prune() invocation starts fresh with a new maxTimestamp that is further in the future, causing data to be pruned out of order and violating the sequential pruning invariant?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: prune()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that causes the DB connection to be returned to the pool in a broken state, causing subsequent calls to recordFileRepository.findLatestWithOffset() to throw a JdbcSQLException that is caught by the outer catch block, silently disabling all pruning for the duration of the partition without any alerting mechanism?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: prune(RecordFileIterator)] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that causes the retentionRepositories.forEach() to fail on the Nth repository in the collection, leaving the first N-1 repositories pruned and the remaining repositories un-pruned within the same transaction, and since the transaction is rolled back, causing the counters to show 0 deletions while the actual DB state is unchanged, masking the failure?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: prune()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that causes the Spring @Scheduled executor to create a new thread for the next scheduled invocation while the previous invocation is still blocked on the synchronized lock, causing thread accumulation in the scheduler pool that eventually exhausts all available threads and causes a JVM-level OutOfMemoryError?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: hasNext()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that causes the DB to return a RecordFile with null consensusEnd from findNextBetween(), causing a NullPointerException when next.getConsensusEnd() is called in prune(RecordFileIterator), which propagates up to the outer catch block and terminates the pruning job with no data deleted?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: prune()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that causes the importer to lose track of which record files have already been pruned, causing the next prune() invocation to recompute maxTimestamp from a post-partition database state that includes newly ingested files, and prune data that was written during the partition recovery window before it has been fully validated?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: prune(RecordFileIterator)] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that causes executeWithoutResult() to throw a CannotCreateTransactionException, which is caught by the outer catch(Exception e) block, causing the iterator's current pointer to remain at the last successfully processed RecordFile, and the next prune() invocation to start a new iterator from scratch, potentially re-pruning already-deleted data ranges?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: prune()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that causes the database to process the DELETE statement from repository.prune(endTimestamp) but fail to send the acknowledgment back to the importer, causing the importer to retry the same DELETE on reconnection and log double the actual deletion count in the counters map?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: hasNext()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that causes recordFileRepository.findNextBetween(endTimestamp, max.getConsensusEnd()) to return a record file with consensusEnd equal to endTimestamp (the exclusive lower bound), violating the query's exclusivity invariant due to a stale replica read, causing the iterator to process the same batch window twice and delete data that should be retained?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: prune()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that causes the Spring transaction manager to use a different database connection for findLatestWithOffset() and for the subsequent executeWithoutResult() block, causing the two operations to see different database states and prune data based on a maxTimestamp that does not correspond to the data visible in the pruning transaction?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: prune(RecordFileIterator)] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that causes the DB connection to be silently dropped during the forEach loop, causing some repository.prune() calls to execute against a stale connection that returns 0 rows deleted without throwing an exception, causing the counters to undercount actual deletions and the rate calculation to be incorrect?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: prune()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that causes the @Scheduled fixedDelayString to fail to parse retentionProperties.getFrequency().toMillis() at bean initialization time, causing the Spring context to fail to start and the entire importer service to be unavailable?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: hasNext()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that causes the DB to return inconsistent results for findNextBetween() across two calls within the same hasNext() invocation (one for initialization and one for batch advancement), causing the iterator to compute a batchPeriod window that spans a gap in the record file sequence and prune data from a time range with no corresponding record files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: prune()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that causes the importer's JPA session cache to become stale, causing findLatestWithOffset() to return a cached RecordFile object whose consensusEnd does not reflect the actual latest value in the database, resulting in the pruning window being computed from outdated data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: prune(RecordFileIterator)] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that causes the transactionOperations.executeWithoutResult() to execute in auto-commit mode due to a transaction manager misconfiguration triggered by the partition, causing each repository.prune() call to be committed independently and making it impossible to roll back partial deletions if a subsequent repository fails?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: prune()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that causes the DB to execute the DELETE query from repository.prune() but hold an exclusive lock on the affected rows for the duration of the partition, blocking all other database operations that need to read or write those rows and causing a cascading lock timeout across the entire mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: hasNext()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that causes the DB to return a RecordFile from findNextBetween() that has a consensusEnd value less than the endTimestamp parameter (violating the query's lower bound), causing the iterator to move backward in time and re-prune already-deleted data ranges?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: prune()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that causes the Spring scheduler to lose track of the fixedDelay timer, causing prune() to be invoked immediately after the previous invocation completes (zero delay), creating a tight loop of pruning operations that saturates the database with DELETE queries and causes a denial-of-service for all other database consumers?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: prune(RecordFileIterator)] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that causes the DB connection to be reset between the counters.merge() call and the actual commit of the transaction, causing the counters to reflect deletions that were never committed, and the log message 'Pruned N entries' to report false data that misleads operators into believing pruning succeeded?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: prune()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that causes the importer to reconnect to a different database node in a cluster with replication lag, causing findLatestWithOffset() to return a record file that is behind the actual latest, computing a maxTimestamp that is in the past and causing the pruning window to be smaller than the configured retentionPeriod?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: hasNext()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that causes the DB to return a RecordFile from findNextBetween() with a consensusEnd that equals max.getConsensusEnd() but is a newly created object (not the same reference as max), causing the current == max identity check to fail and the iterator to call findNextBetween() again with endTimestamp = max.getConsensusEnd() + batchPeriod, which exceeds max and causes current to be set to max again in an infinite loop?
